===''Pokémon Horizons: The Series''=== Kabu made his debut at the end of ''[[HZ019|The Bittersweet Truth]]'', where he was seen eating at the Motostoke [[Battle Café]]. In the [[HZ020|next episode]], he made his way to [[Motostoke Stadium]] where [[Liko]] and [[Roy]] joined him to ask about the [[Black Rayquaza]]. Wanting to assist the two young Trainers, he, Kabu had them undergo a {{pkmn|training}} session that helped them find new methods of using their Pokémon's most basic [[Move|attacks]] by using them to either light up or extinguish the flames of a group of {{p|Litwick}}. [[File:Kabu Rayquaza flashback.png|thumb|250px|left|Kabu seeing the [[Black Rayquaza]]]] After Roy had won the challenge, Kabu challenged the two to a [[Multi Battle]], with his {{tc|Gym Trainer}} apprentice {{OBP|Wakaba|HZ020}} as his partner. Kabu's {{p|Centiskorch}} easily overwhelmed both {{TP|Liko|Sprigatito}} and {{TP|Roy|Fuecoco}}, even when the latter learned {{m|Flamethrower}} mid-battle. Once Fuecoco had been defeated, Kabu left the battle against Liko to Wakaba. After Liko's unexpected surrender, Kabu told her that while being kind and compassionate is a good thing, being compassionate to this degree during a battle can be disrespectful toward both the opponent and the participating Pokémon, as both Wakaba and Sprigatito were disappointed in her not finishing the battle in earnest. When Liko and Roy later left, Kabu encouraged Liko to find her own path in life. In ''[[HZ023|Fiery Galarian Moltres]]'', Kabu went to the [[Galar Mine]] to investigate the reports of a collapse that happened there. Upon arriving there, he found Liko, Roy, and [[Friede]], who revealed that the chaos had been caused by a {{rf|Galarian}} {{TP|Lucius|Moltres}}. When they left, Kabu realized that Friede was trying to hide {{an|Terapagos|something}}, but simply smiled and let them go. Kabu appeared in a flashback to Liko and Roy's journey in ''[[HZ045|From So Far Away]]''. Kabu reappeared in a flashback in ''[[HZ085|The Pokémon Paradise of Laqua!]]''. ====Character==== Kabu was shown to be a very wise and mature person, highly respected in his hometown. He is shown doing athletic activities like jogging throughout the anime. He also gave Liko several pieces of advice on how to train her {{TP|Liko|Sprigatito}}. {{-}} ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Kabu's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|HZ}}: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Kabu |pkmn=Centiskorch |type1=Fire |type2=Bug |img=Kabu Centiskorch.png |epnum=HZ020 |epname=Kabu's Battle Training! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Centiskorch}} is Kabu's only known Pokémon. He used it in a [[Multi Battle]] against [[Liko]] and [[Roy]], teaming up with {{OBP|Wakaba|HZ020}}'s {{p|Rolycoly}}. Centiskorch ended up defeating [[Roy's Fuecoco]] before withdrawing from the battle. Centiskorch's known moves are {{m|Flame Wheel}}, {{m|Bug Bite}}, and {{m|Flamethrower}}, and its [[Ability]] is {{a|Flash Fire}}.}} ====Artwork==== {| style="margin:auto; text-align:center; {{roundy|20px}} border:2px solid #{{fire color dark}}; background:#{{fire color}}; font-size:80%; color:#fff;" |- | style="{{roundy|15px}} border: 2px solid #{{fire color dark}}; background: #{{fire color light}};"| [[File:Pokémon Horizons Promotional Poster 2.jpg|x200px]] | style="{{roundy|15px}} border: 2px solid #{{fire color dark}}; background: #{{fire color light}};"| [[File:Kabu HZ key artwork.png|x200px]] | style="{{roundy|5px}} border:2px solid #{{fire color dark}}; background:#{{fire color light}}; width:80px"| [[File:Kabu HZ expression sheet.png|x100px]] | style="{{roundy|5px}} border:2px solid #{{fire color dark}}; background:#{{fire color light}}; width:80px"| [[File:HZ September Calendar.png|x250px]] | style="{{roundy|5px}} border:2px solid #{{fire color dark}}; background:#{{fire color light}}; width:80px"| [[File:HZ eyecatch Kabu Centiskorch.png|x150px]] |- | Official poster from
